objective: Switch worker to live Supabase writes and confirm rows land in founder_alpha.fa_window_capture

credentials.normalisation_reason: supabase-js appends /rest/v1 itself; the given URL would have produced /rest/v1/rest/v1/

evidence.public_schema_probe: PGRST205 table-not-found (NOT an auth error) => PostgREST reachable and key accepted
evidence.postgrest_message: Only the following schemas are exposed: public, graphql_public

root_cause: The founder_alpha schema exists and is verified (CTO applied migration) but is not in the PostgREST exposed-schemas list, so the REST API refuses to serve it.

remediation.owner: Founder or CTO (Supabase dashboard setting; cannot be changed via service-role key)
steps: Save (PostgREST reloads its schema cache automatically)
Then restart the worker WITHOUT --dry-run for live writes
Optionally: node scripts/backfill.js --dry  then without --dry to load captured JSONL

interim_action: Worker restarted in dry-run mode (PID recorded in logs/worker-live-pending.log). Capture continues to data/capture/*.jsonl with zero data loss; scripts/backfill.js loads it once the schema is exposed.
robustness_fix: Sink now spills to data/spill/*.jsonl after 3 consecutive write failures or >5000 pending rows, bounding memory. Previously an outage would have grown the queue until OOM and lost everything in memory. Proven by test: 10 rows spilled, pending returned to 0, zero rows lost.
